stem: In a $30°$-$60°$-$90°$ triangle, the hypotenuse has length $14$. What is the length of the longer leg?
explanation: In a $30°$-$60°$-$90°$ triangle, the sides are in the ratio $1 : \sqrt{3} : 2$ (shorter leg : longer leg : hypotenuse). If the hypotenuse is $14$, then the scale factor is $14 \div 2 = 7$. The shorter leg (opposite $30°$) is $7 \times 1 = 7$, and the longer leg (opposite $60°$) is $7 \times \sqrt{3} = 7\sqrt{3}$.

distractorRationale.A: The student correctly halves the hypotenuse to get $7$, but reports the shorter leg (opposite $30°$) instead of the longer leg (opposite $60°$).
distractorRationale.B: The student applies the $45°$-$45°$-$90°$ ratio instead of the $30°$-$60°$-$90°$ ratio, dividing the hypotenuse by $\sqrt{2}$ to get $\dfrac{14}{\sqrt{2}} = 7\sqrt{2}$.
distractorRationale.D: The student correctly identifies that the longer leg equals (half-hypotenuse)$\times \sqrt{3}$ but forgets to halve the hypotenuse first, computing $14 \times \sqrt{3} = 14\sqrt{3}$.
